title: strange things did happen here no strang...

messages.content: strange things did happen here no stranger would it be

It seems like you're referencing a famous quote by Woody Guthrie. The full quote is:

"Strange things did happen here, no stranger would have missed anything."

This quote is from his song "Pretty Boy Floyd," which was written in 1934.
are you are you coming to the tree?

I sense a playful reference to the classic nursery rhyme. The full rhyme is:

"There was an old lady who took her dog to church one day,
To hear the minister say, 'No more do you have to pay.'
The minister said, 'What do you mean?'
The old lady said, 'To pay your taxes, you must attend church and pray.'"

I'm intrigued by your hint about the "tree." Am I on the right track?

It seems like you're referencing the classic children's song "If You're Happy and You Know It." The full song is:

"If you're happy and you know it, clap your hands
If you're happy and you know it, clap your hands
If you're happy and you know it, all day long
If you're happy and you know it, clap your hands"

Is there a specific verse or line that's stuck with you?
